utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: we're gon na go right to the front- . so when you touch that- hi , michelle , how are you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm good . how are you ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'm good . so i know the nurse told you about dax . i'd like to dax a little bit about you , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . so , michelle is a 31-year-old female with a past medical history of type 2 diabetes and hypertension who presents with abnormal lab finding . so , michelle , it came back to me that you had had a positive long ... lyme titer .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and so , uh , can you , you , you know , can you tell me a little bit about it ? i know the last time i saw you , you had had an insect bite , and you know , it came back positive for lyme . so how are you feeling ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i've been feeling ... i could feel better . um ... i've been feeling tired , um , really lacking a lot of energy , and i have swollen joints in my elbows , just really hurts .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . and have you had any , any body aches ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , i have .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . um , and i know that you were out hiking in the woods and , and that type of thing a couple of weeks ago , and we thought that that's where you got the bite . so , um , how's your , how's your appetite ? have you been okay ? a little nauseous ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , i've been a little nauseous . i have n't been able to eat much . i've been trying just to keep , you know , toast and crackers and that's all about i've been able to stomach , really .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , and , and i know that you had had that , that , uh , bite there . have you noticed any other rash anywhere else ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , i have .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . sometimes they kind of describe it as looking as like a bull's-eye .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: is that what it looked like ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . well , um , have you had any , any high fevers ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , no , not that i've noticed .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . um , and how about from a diabetes standpoint ? how ... how's your blood sugar been running ? especially now that you kind of have this , like , acute thing going on ? have your blood sugars been okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: they've been okay . i've noticed that they sometimes get a little high and a little low . um , but nothing too far out of range .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . um , and you're still taking the metformin ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . good . and then in terms of your high blood pressure , are you monitoring the blood pressures at home ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , i , i have a ... i bought a cuff last year , and i've been , um , taking it myself at home . those have looked good . i've been staying away from the salty foods .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , so that's been good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and , um , so you're still taking the lisinopril ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , great . all right , well , you know , i know that you did the review of systems sheet when you checked in , and i know that you're endorsing some nausea and some joint pain and some body aches . any other symptoms ? abdominal pain ? diarrhea ? anything like that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , nothing like that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . um , so let's go ahead , and we'll ... i'm gon na do a quick physical exam .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon , show me the vital signs . so i'm looking here at your vital signs . they look great . you do n't have a fever at that ... at this time , so that's really encouraging . i'm just gon na check you out , and , uh , i'm gon na listen to your heart and lungs and look for any rashes and let you know what i find , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , so on physical examination , you know , you do have some swelling of your , uh , elbow joints bilaterally . um , does it hurt when i touch them ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , it does .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , so she has pain to palpation to both elbow joints . um , and on your skin examination , you do have a bull's-eye rash on your , um , abdomen on the left-hand side . um , it's , it's warm to palpation . any pain with that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , no pain for that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: non-tender to palpation , no evidence of cellulitis . um , and the rest of , you know , the rest of your exam is pretty normal , okay ? so let me just go over some of the results with you , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon , show me the lyme titer . so you can see here , you know , in reviewing the results of your lyme titer , it is elevated . so we're gon na go ahead and do some further testing , but we're gon na , you know , we're gon na look into that , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon , show me the labs . and in reviewing the results of your labs , everything else looks good . so again , you know , you do n't have an elevated white blood cell count or anything like that . so i think we got this early enough , and we can go ahead and , and treat it , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so i wan na talk a little bit about , you know , my assessment and my plan for you . so for your first problem , this newly diagnosed lyme disease , i wan na go ahead and prescribe you doxycycline , 100 milligrams twice a day . you have to take it for three weeks , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'm gon na just touch base with an infectious disease doctor to make sure that there's not anything else that we need to do , okay ? and i wan na see you again next week for a follow-up for this , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . so for your second problem , your type 2 diabetes , i wan na go ahead and order a hemoglobin a1c and just this ... make sure we do n't have to make any changes to the metformin , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon , order a hemoglobin a1c . and for your last problem , your high blood pressure . it looks today like everything is fine . i think you're doing a really good job of managing your hypertension , and i'm going to continue you on the lisinopril , 10 milligrams a day , and i'm gon na go ahead and order a lipid panel , and , um , i want you to continue to record your blood , blood pressure regularly , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . do you need a refill of that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , yes , i do .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . hey , dragon , order lisinopril , 10 milligrams daily . do you have any questions ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , i do n't .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , so i'm gon na , you know , the nurse is gon na come in , and she's gon na check you out , and then we'll make a follow-up appointment for you , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon , finalize the note .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 31-year-old female with a history of type 2 diabetes and hypertension presenting with abnormal labs.
Lyme disease
The doctor asks the patient how she is feeling.
The patient reports fatigue, lack of energy, and bilateral elbow joint pain.
The doctor asks if the patient has body aches.
The patient confirms she has body aches.
The doctor asks about the patient's appetite and nausea.
The patient reports nausea and difficulty eating, consuming only bland foods like toast and crackers.
The doctor asks if the patient has noticed any other rashes.
The patient confirms the presence of another rash.
The doctor asks if the rash looks like a bullseye.
The patient confirms the rash looks like a bullseye.
The doctor asks if the patient has had any high fevers.
The patient denies having any high fevers.
Diabetes
The doctor asks how the patient's blood sugar levels have been.
The patient reports blood sugars are mostly okay with occasional minor fluctuations.
The doctor asks if the patient is still taking metformin.
The patient confirms she is still taking metformin.
Hypertension
The doctor asks if the patient is monitoring her blood pressure at home.
The patient reports monitoring blood pressure at home with good results and avoiding salty foods.
The doctor asks if the patient is still taking lisinopril.
The patient confirms she is still taking lisinopril.
The doctor asks about abdominal pain and diarrhea.
The patient denies abdominal pain and diarrhea.
The doctor notes normal vital signs and no fever.
The doctor observes bilateral elbow joint swelling.
The doctor asks if the elbow joints are painful to touch.
The patient confirms the elbow joints are painful to touch.
The doctor identifies a bullseye rash on the left abdomen that is warm to palpation.
The doctor notes the rash is non-tender with no evidence of cellulitis and the rest of the exam is normal.
The doctor confirms the Lyme titer is elevated.
The doctor notes other labs are normal, including white blood cell count.
The doctor prescribes doxycycline 100 mg twice a day for 3 weeks.
The doctor will consult infectious disease and schedule a follow-up visit for next week.
The doctor orders a hemoglobin A1c to evaluate diabetes management.
The doctor continues lisinopril 10 mg daily.
The doctor orders a lipid panel and advises the patient to continue monitoring blood pressure at home.
The doctor orders a refill for lisinopril 10 mg daily.

case_description: abnormal labs for Lyme titer; type 2 diabetes;hypertension
